title: otholobium striatum
intro: Otholobium striatum, also known as the Striped Pea, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family. It has been traditionally used in South African folk medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in alkaloids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its health benefits, such as relieving pain and reducing inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
